description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver authoring.

## Problem

`lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:411` — the production mix task constructs the MutationRunner input with a literal `test_modules: []`. `MutationLoop`'s per-site `Enum.each(test_modules, &ExUnit.Server.add_module/2)` is therefore a no-op against the empty list, so `ExUnit.run/0` inside the mutation pass reports zero tests run, and EVERY mutation is classified `:survived`.

## Reproduction

Run `mix mutagen --scope lib/foo.ex --tests test/foo_test.exs` against any module that has live mutators: `mutation.results[*].status` is uniformly `"survived"` regardless of whether the cited tests would actually kill those mutations.

## E2E driver workaround

The S7 driver's `mutation_collaborator/1` does an AST walk of each cited `_test.exs`, finds every `defmodule` block, and populates `test_modules` with `[{mod, %{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}}]` entries before delegating to `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`. The production caller still bypasses this.

## Spec reference

`mutagen.mutation_pipeline` r3 (mutation classification semantics) and r5 (`:killed` vs `:survived` semantics) — these rules cannot be exercised by the production wire-up while every result is forced to `:survived`.

## Resolution

In `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` around line 411, derive `test_modules` from the resolved `TestFilter.files` (or equivalent) rather than hardcoding `[]`. Retire the `mutation_collaborator` fork in `end_to_end_test.exs` once the production code is fixed.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 end-to-end suite, where this was discovered)
- mutagen-wrd.11 (TestSelector exclude: [:test] fix — same wire-up surface)
title: DISCOVERY: mix mutagen passes hardcoded test_modules: [] to MutationRunner
